channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 201720
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
1100 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Fausto, located several hundred miles southwest of the
southern tip of Baja California. The National Hurricane Center has
issued the last advisory on Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida, located
well west-northwest of the southern tip of the Baja California
Peninsula.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A broad area of low pressure is forecast to develop well south of
Mexico by midweek. Environmental conditions appear conducive for
development of the system thereafter, and a tropical depression
could form while it moves westward to west-northwestward across the
eastern and central portions of the eastern Pacific.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...medium...60 percent.

Southwest of Hawaii:
Showers and thunderstorms associated with a trough of low pressure
located well southwest of the Hawaiian Islands remain disorganized.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system as it moves generally westward during the
next several days.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...low...20 percent.

$$
Forecaster Katz/Cangialosi
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO EXPECTED TO BECOME A HURRICANE BY TUESDAY... As of 2:00 PM PDT Mon Jul 20 the center of Fausto was located near 14.2, -116.7 with movement NW at 10 mph. The minimum central pressure was 994 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 70 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Fausto
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...FAUSTO EXPECTED TO BECOME A HURRICANE BY TUESDAY...

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Public Advisory Number 8
item.description:
Issued at 200 PM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 202046
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Fausto Advisory Number   8
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
200 PM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
...FAUSTO EXPECTED TO BECOME A HURRICANE BY TUESDAY...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 200 PM PDT...2100 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...14.2N 116.7W
ABOUT 745 MI...1205 KM SW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...70 MPH...110 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...NW OR 310 DEGREES AT 10 MPH...17 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...994 MB...29.36 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 200 PM PDT (2100 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Fausto was 
located near latitude 14.2 North, longitude 116.7 West. Fausto is 
moving toward the northwest near 10 mph (17 km/h). A decrease in 
forward speed and a motion toward the northwest is expected to 
continue for the next day or two.  A turn back to the west-northwest 
is expected on Wednesday.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 70 mph (110 km/h) with higher 
gusts.  Steady to rapid strengthening is expected during the next 
couple of days, and Fausto is forecast to become a hurricane later 
today or tonight.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 140 miles (220 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 994 mb (29.36 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Fausto are expected to reach the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula on Wednesday, and persist for
a couple of days.  These swells are likely to cause life-threatening
surf and rip current conditions.  Please consult products from your
local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 800 PM PDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Katz/Hagen
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 8
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
038 
WTPZ21 KNHC 202045
TCMEP1
 
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER   8
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
2100 UTC MON JUL 20 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 14.2N 116.7W AT 20/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTHWEST OR 310 DEGREES AT   9 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  994 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  60 KT WITH GUSTS TO  75 KT.
50 KT....... 40NE  40SE   0SW  30NW.
34 KT....... 80NE 120SE 120SW  50NW.
4 M SEAS....150NE 150SE  90SW  90NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 14.2N 116.7W AT 20/2100Z
AT 20/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 14.0N 116.6W
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0600Z 15.1N 117.9W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
64 KT... 15NE  15SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT... 40NE  40SE   0SW  20NW.
34 KT...100NE 140SE 130SW  70NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1800Z 16.0N 119.4W
MAX WIND  80 KT...GUSTS 100 KT.
64 KT... 15NE  10SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 40NE  40SE  20SW  30NW.
34 KT...120NE 150SE 120SW  80NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0600Z 16.6N 120.8W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
64 KT... 20NE  20SE  10SW  20NW.
50 KT... 50NE  50SE  30SW  40NW.
34 KT...130NE 140SE 110SW 100NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1800Z 17.0N 122.4W
MAX WIND  95 KT...GUSTS 115 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  30SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 50NE  60SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE 140SE 110SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0600Z 17.4N 124.5W
MAX WIND  95 KT...GUSTS 115 KT.
64 KT... 40NE  40SE  20SW  40NW.
50 KT... 70NE  70SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 150SE 110SW 140NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1800Z 17.7N 127.2W
MAX WIND  90 KT...GUSTS 110 KT.
64 KT... 35NE  25SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 60NE  70SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 140SE 110SW 140NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 24/1800Z 18.5N 132.6W
MAX WIND  80 KT...GUSTS 100 KT.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 130SE 110SW 140NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 25/1800Z 19.4N 137.7W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
50 KT... 50NE  40SE  30SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE 110SE 100SW 130NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 14.2N 116.7W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 21/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER KATZ/HAGEN
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 7
item.description:
Issued at 800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
000
WTPZ41 KNHC 201435
TCDEP1
 
Tropical Storm Fausto Discussion Number   7
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
Deep convection has been increasing in strength and coverage over
the past several hours, and Fausto continues to gradually gain
strength.  The low-level center, although difficult to locate, is
still near the northern edge of the thunderstorm activity due to
north-northeasterly vertical wind shear.  The initial intensity has
been nudged up to 55 kt, which is near the high end of the latest
satellite estimates.
 
The shear is expected to decrease, and since Fausto is forecast to
remain over warm waters and in a moist environment, continued
strengthening is forecast.  In fact, the SHIPS model suggests that
there is about 1 in 3 chance that Fausto could rapidly intensify.
This seems plausible, especially since it appears to have a
developing compact core in satellite images.  The NHC intensity
forecast remains at the high end of the model guidance and shows
Fausto becoming a hurricane tonight and approaching major hurricane
status in a couple of days.  Gradual weakening is expected toward
the end of the week once Fausto moves into an environment of
progressively cooler waters and drier air.
 
The storm is slowly gaining latitude, with the initial motion
estimated to be 295/11 kt.  Fausto will likely move toward the
northwest and slow down during the next day or two as it tracks
toward a weakness in the ridge left behind by Elida.  By Wednesday,
however, the global models show the ridge strengthening northeast of
the system, and that should cause Fausto to move faster to the
west-northwest and continue in that direction into the weekend.  The
NHC track forecast is very similar to the previous one and roughly
near the middle of the guidance envelope.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  20/1500Z 13.5N 116.4W   55 KT  65 MPH
 12H  21/0000Z 14.4N 117.5W   65 KT  75 MPH
 24H  21/1200Z 15.4N 119.0W   75 KT  85 MPH
 36H  22/0000Z 16.1N 120.6W   85 KT 100 MPH
 48H  22/1200Z 16.6N 122.1W   95 KT 110 MPH
 60H  23/0000Z 16.9N 124.0W   95 KT 110 MPH
 72H  23/1200Z 17.3N 126.3W   90 KT 105 MPH
 96H  24/1200Z 18.0N 131.5W   80 KT  90 MPH
120H  25/1200Z 19.1N 136.7W   70 KT  80 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Cangialosi
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 7
item.description:
Issued at 1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 201434
PWSEP1
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER   7           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 1500Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 13.5 NORTH...LONGITUDE 116.4 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 55 KTS...65 MPH...100 KM/H.                              
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        12Z MON AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -

  TIME       12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 12Z WED 12Z THU 12Z FRI
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 12Z WED 12Z THU 12Z FRI 12Z SAT
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
15N 120W       34  X  67(67)  21(88)   1(89)   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)
15N 120W       50  X   5( 5)  22(27)   1(28)   X(28)   X(28)   X(28)
15N 120W       64  X   1( 1)  10(11)   X(11)   X(11)   X(11)   X(11)
 
15N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  21(22)   X(22)   X(22)
15N 125W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
15N 125W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   X( 2)   X( 2)
 
20N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  16(16)   1(17)   X(17)
 
15N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   9(10)   X(10)
15N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   X( 2)
15N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   X( 1)
 
20N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)  37(39)   1(40)
20N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  10(10)   X(10)
20N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   1( 4)
 
15N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   5( 6)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   8( 8)  49(57)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  23(24)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  10(11)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  20(20)
20N 140W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)
20N 140W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Graphics
item.description: Wind Speed Probabilities last updated Mon, 20 Jul 2026 15:33:33 GMT

item.title: Summary for Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida (EP5/EP052026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...ELIDA BECOMES POST-TROPICAL... ...DANGEROUS SURF AND HIGH RISK OF RIP CURRENTS IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA CONTINUES... As of 8:00 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 the center of Elida was located near 26.4, -127.2 with movement NNW at 13 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1000 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 40 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Elida
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...ELIDA BECOMES POST-TROPICAL... ...DANGEROUS SURF AND HIGH RISK OF RIP CURRENTS IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA CONTINUES...

item.title: Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Public Advisory Number 24
item.description:
Issued at 800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
000
WTPZ35 KNHC 201434
TCPEP5
 
BULLETIN
Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Advisory Number  24
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
...ELIDA BECOMES POST-TROPICAL...
...DANGEROUS SURF AND HIGH RISK OF RIP CURRENTS IN SOUTHERN
CALIFORNIA CONTINUES...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 800 AM PDT...1500 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...26.4N 127.2W
ABOUT 1110 MI...1790 KM WNW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...40 MPH...65 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...NNW OR 345 DEGREES AT 13 MPH...20 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1000 MB...29.53 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 800 AM PDT (1500 UTC), the center of Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida 
was located near latitude 26.4 North, longitude 127.2 West. The 
post-tropical cyclone is moving toward the north-northwest near 13 
mph (20 km/h). A turn to the north with an increase in forward speed 
is forecast the during the next couple days.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 40 mph (65 km/h) with higher gusts.
The post-tropical cyclone is expected to continue to weaken and 
should dissipate within the next couple of days.

Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 140 miles (220 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1000 mb (29.53 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Elida will affect portions of the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula and southern California
during the next several days. These swells will likely result in
life-threatening surf and rip current conditions. Please consult
products from your local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
This is the last public advisory issued by the National Hurricane 
Center on this system. For additional information please see High 
Seas Forecasts issued by the National Weather Service, under AWIPS 
header NFDHSFEPI, WMO header FZPN02 KWBC, and on the web at 
ocean.weather.gov/shtml/NFDHSFEPI.php

$$
Forecaster Katz/Cangialosi
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Forecast Advisory Number 24
item.description:
Issued at 1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
WTPZ25 KNHC 201434
TCMEP5
 
POST-TROPICAL CYCLONE ELIDA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  24
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP052026
1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026
 
POST-TROPICAL CYCLONE CENTER LOCATED NEAR 26.4N 127.2W AT 20/1500Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTH-NORTHWEST OR 345 DEGREES AT  11 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1000 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  35 KT WITH GUSTS TO  45 KT.
34 KT.......120NE  40SE   0SW   0NW.
4 M SEAS....180NE 180SE 300SW 180NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 26.4N 127.2W AT 20/1500Z
AT 20/1200Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 25.7N 127.1W
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0000Z 28.5N 127.4W...POST-TROPICAL
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT... 80NE   0SE   0SW  70NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1200Z 31.1N 127.6W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0000Z 33.6N 127.4W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  25 KT...GUSTS  35 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1200Z 35.6N 127.1W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  20 KT...GUSTS  30 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0000Z 37.7N 126.6W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  15 KT...GUSTS  20 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1200Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 26.4N 127.2W
 
THIS IS THE LAST FORECAST/ADVISORY ISSUED BY THE NATIONAL HURRICANE 
CENTER ON THIS SYSTEM. FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION PLEASE SEE HIGH 
SEAS FORECASTS ISSUED BY THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE...UNDER AWIPS 
HEADER NFDHSFEPI AND WMO HEADER FZPN02 KWBC.
 
$$
FORECASTER KATZ/CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Forecast Discussion Number 24
item.description:
Issued at 800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
000
WTPZ45 KNHC 201435
TCDEP5
 
Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Discussion Number  24
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
Elida no longer possesses any deep convection near the center and it 
has not produced any significant convection since about 0400 UTC. As 
Elida moves over even colder sea-surface temperatures, and 
encounters more drier air and wind shear, the remaining circulation 
should dissipate within the next couple of days. The initial 
intensity is lowered to 35 kt, based on the TAFB subjective Dvorak 
fix of 2.5/35 kt.

Elida has turned toward the north-northwest and appears to be 
increasing its forward speed, with the latest motion estimated at 
345/10 kt. This north-northwestward then northward motion should 
continue during the next few days as the cyclone moves between a 
deep-layer trough to its northwest and a ridge centered over the 
southwestern United States. The NHC track forecast continues to be 
near the previous track, and lies near the corrected consensus HCCA.

Gradual weakening is expected to continue over the next couple of 
days as the hostile environmental conditions persist, with the 
cyclone forecast to dissipate into an open trough within a few 
days. 

This is the last NHC advisory on Elida.  For additional information 
please see High Seas Forecasts issued by the National Weather 
Service, under AWIPS header NFDHSFEPI, WMO header FZPN02 KWBC, and 
on the web at ocean.weather.gov/shtml/NFDHSFEPI.php
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  20/1500Z 26.4N 127.2W   35 KT  40 MPH...POST-TROPICAL
 12H  21/0000Z 28.5N 127.4W   35 KT  40 MPH...POST-TROPICAL
 24H  21/1200Z 31.1N 127.6W   30 KT  35 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 36H  22/0000Z 33.6N 127.4W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 48H  22/1200Z 35.6N 127.1W   20 KT  25 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 60H  23/0000Z 37.7N 126.6W   15 KT  15 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 72H  23/1200Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Katz
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Wind Speed Probabilities Number 24
item.description:
Issued at 1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
FOPZ15 KNHC 201435
PWSEP5
                                                                    
POST-TROPICAL CYCLONE ELIDA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  24     
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP052026               
1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 1500Z THE CENTER OF POST-TROPICAL CYCLONE ELIDA WAS LOCATED NEAR 
LATITUDE 26.4 NORTH...LONGITUDE 127.2 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 35 KTS...40 MPH...65 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        12Z MON AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 12Z WED 12Z THU 12Z FRI
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 12Z WED 12Z THU 12Z FRI 12Z SAT
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
